title: Butternut Squash & Marmite Gnocchi Traybake
summary: This cheesy butternut squash gnocchi bake will give your fave mac and cheese recipe a real run for its money. Easy, cheesy goodness.

ingredients.label: 800g Frozen Butternut Squash Cubes or 1 Butternut Squash, Peeled and Cut Into 3cm Cubes
ingredients.name: Butternut Squash

method.description: Preheat the oven to 200°C. Tip the squash cubes into a large roasting tray, drizzle with 1/3 of the olive oil. Season with salt and pepper and toss well. Snuggle the unpeeled garlic cloves into the tray, then roast for 30 mins, until the cubes are soft.

Squeeze the flesh from the garlic cloves into a blender with half of the squash cubes. Tip the gnocchi into the tray with the remaining squash cubes and slide back into the oven and bake for 20 mins further.
Meanwhile, add the cream cheese to the blender and grate the Parmesan in. Add the boiling water, the marmite and juice of ½ a lemon. Season with salt and pepper and blend to a creamy sauce.

Add 2/3 of the olive oil to a frying pan over a medium-high heat. Line a plate with a sheet of kitchen paper. Add the sage leaves to the hot oil and cook for 2 mins, until dark green and crisp, then remove with a slotted spoon onto the lined plate. Add the chilli flakes to the pan of oil and set aside.
Remove the bake from the oven, cover with the squash sauce and mix, adding a little more water to loosen it if necessary. Scatter with the crispy sage leaves and chilli oil to serve.

chefs.name: Ben Lippett
chefs.summary: Ben Lippett (@dinnerbyben) is a cook, food writer, recipe developer, and co-founder of Dr Sting's Hot Honey. Ben spent years working at a spate of restaurants in the UK and Australia – including a brief but rewarding stint at a butcher's – before making the leap to food media.
